title: Northbridge Sovereign Dataworks
preview: EU-headquartered fintech. Primary ask: Private VPC deployment with strict EU-only data residency for model inputs/outputs and audit trail. Secondary: selective UK and Canada lanes for localized products (must be opt-in). Strong requirement for customer-managed KMS (HSM) and end-to-end audit logging. SSO/SAML + SCIM required. Interested in Redwood Private (VPC) with Dedicated fallback for burst. Cost sensitivity moderate — willing to commit if SLOs + residency are guaranteed. Legal pushed back on DPA wording re: cross-border transfers. Quote: 'We cannot tolerate token-level residency leakage — full chain must be auditable.'

title: Ironbark Homeland Knowledge
preview:
Top-line: federal homeland agency evaluating on-prem/air-gapped RAG platform for classified document search. High procurement overhead; must pass JITC-style security checklist and formal red-team. Prospect has existing on-prem ELK + custom BM25 baseline but wants embeddings + reranking for improved precision at scale.

Recent timeline:
- 2025-09-18: inbound contact from technology acquisition team (initial qualification). AE assigned (Ethan P.).
- 2025-10-02: discovery call w/ CTO office (Maya joined). Captured scale: ~1.2B documents across agencies, live ingestion from records mgmt and scanned OCR pipelines.
- 2025-12-07: architecture workshop — requirement: no outbound egress from classified network; model artifacts must be packaged, signed, and verified.
- 2026-01-19: POC scoping meeting. Agreed POC = 100M-doc index on isolated cluster, vector index + lightweight ANN, reranking model to run in the same air-gapped enclave.
- 2026-02-14: kickoff call (linked_fireflies ff_2026-02-14_ironbark_intro). Confirmed lead security reviewer and procurement POC.
- 2026-02-27: demo of embedding generator (quantized LLM variant) and reranker latency test in lab; latency hits: embedding gen 12–18ms/token on A100-equivalent VM, ANN query 20–40ms per query for 100k-shard config. SE notes: needs sub-200ms end-to-end for short queries.

Requirements / preferences (from notes):
- Workload: high-volume search + guided discovery for analysts (RAG flows, citation + provenance). Primary = embeddings + ANN + transformer reranker. Secondary = summarization for exportable briefs.
- Scale: target index 500M–1.5B vectors, incremental daily ingestion 1–5M docs. Must support sharding/partitioning across classified enclaves.
- Latency SLO: median <150ms for 95th percentile queries in typical analyst scenario; budget 100–300ms depending on reranker complexity.
- Throughput: bursty (analyst spikes), typical steady qps 200–800, peak 1500+ for incident response.
- Cost sensitivity: procurement cares about TCO across private fleet; model quantization + batching recommendations valued; prefer clear unit economics.
- Models: prefer vetted open models; must be able to pin model artifacts (no outbound pulls). Reranker can be distilled/quantized but must pass accuracy checks.
- Routing/fallback: offline fallback to BM25 when reranker unavailable; deterministic degrade path required.

Security & compliance notes:
- Air-gapped deployment mandatory for classified data lanes. Key management via on-prem HSM; KMS integrations must be local.
- Audit logging and immutable event trail required for all inference calls. Retention controls mandated by data classification (configurable).
- SSO/SAML integration for unclassified consoles; elevated access flows require 2-person review.

POC plan (agreed):
- Phase 0 (pre-POC): deliver sealed model bundle + binary checksum, security questionnaire (week).
- Phase 1 (2 weeks): index 100M docs, baseline ANN perf, verify end-to-end <250ms on our lab hardware.
- Phase 2 (4 weeks): scale to 300M, run reranker A/B vs BM25, measure precision@5, false positive rate, cost per query.
- Phase 3 (4 weeks): hardened packaging for air-gapped deployment + ops runbook + SLA proposal.

Commercial/process notes:
- Buying process: multi-stage government procurement; SOW + security attestation -> PO -> hardware certification -> contract. Procurement expects months; forecast close target 2026-06.
- Pricing sensitivity: they asked for committed pricing tiers for 1yr and 3yr on dedicated private deployment; want cap on annual token cost for redaction/sanitization transforms.

Quotes / paraphrases captured:
- "We need predictable tail latency — analysts can't wait for reranker timeouts."
- "No network egress — everything must be verifiable and auditable on-prem."

Open items / blockers:
- Legal needs exact language for model artifact custody in contract.
- Red-team schedule TBD; procurement board calendar slow through May.
- Need matrix of HW profiles (min GPU/SKU + recommended cache sizes) for POC signoff. SE action: Maya to produce hardware sizing + cost per query estimate by 2026-03-10.

Next steps for AE/SE:
- Ethan: finalize SOW draft and share with procurement (drive://redwood/proposals/ironbark-2026/pricing-deck-v3.pdf).
- Maya: deliver HW sizing + perf projection (include quantized model benchmarks).
- Schedule red-team submission window; coordinate with security lead (contact: seclead@ironbark-hk.gov).

Attachments / artifacts referenced:
- Pricing deck v3 (drive link), security FAQ draft, Fireflies transcript ID above, two support tickets for pre-POC TLS/HSM questions (RL-11209, RL-11345).

title: Acme AI
preview:
Account summary
- Existing Hosted API customer (self-serve -> sales-assisted expansion in late 2025). Heavy chat + some embeddings for internal knowledge base.
- Cost sensitive; measures $/resolved ticket and strongly cares about predictable unit economics.

Pricing / packaging experiment exposure
- Flagged into 2026Q1 pricing experiment cohort (Variant B) for a subset of workspaces starting ~2026-01-22.
  - Experiment tag in telemetry: EXP_PP_2026Q1_VARIANT_B (per Product Ops).
  - Customer noticed plan messaging + overage behavior differences across workspaces; asked for a single consolidated statement for Finance.
- Cohort caveat: meter fixes overlapped with experiment window; any usage deltas need pre/post segmentation in readouts.

Recent activity timeline (most recent first)
- 2026-02-25: Renewal / expansion check-in. Acme asked for spend forecast with "billed tokens" separated from cache hits. Wants commitment options (Dedicated) if it reduces variance.
- 2026-02-12: Internal: Finance pulled Acme into discrepancy sample set for Feb preview run (see invoice-discrepancy-samples-feb-2026).
- 2026-02-06: Call: Pricing & usage review (Fireflies link). Key quote: "We’re not trying to nickel-and-dime—just need the invoice to reconcile to what our engineers see."
- 2026-02-04 to 2026-02-10: Slack triage thread on invoice discrepancy and suspected retry double-counting; Eng confirmed issue pattern matched streaming retry/fallback path; fix queued.

Billing questions / issues (open + resolved)
1) Invoice tokens did not match usage API export (SUP-28451)
- Symptom: Daily export tokens ~2–4% lower than invoice preview for certain days/models; variance clustered by region.
- Current hypothesis (as of Feb): missing/incorrect region dimension in usage events caused pricing attribution drift in internal aggregation vs customer export slice.
- Mitigation: send reconciled export keyed on ledger aggregation + corrected region dimension once backfill is complete; document that "usage API" is source-of-truth for customer reporting but invoice is computed from billing ledger.

2) Suspected double-charged retries / fallback (SUP-28463)
- Symptom: Customer saw spikes during incidents where Redwood performed server-side retry/fallback; they believed they were billed twice for partial streams.
- Eng notes: streaming retry/fallback path could emit duplicate usage events under partial failure. Fix landed in Feb rollout (see PR references in internal threads).
- Status: Pending Finance decision on whether Feb invoice needs a goodwill credit vs strict forward-only correction. Acme explicitly asked for a credit if we confirm duplicate billing.

3) Token counting fairness (streaming + tool calls)
- Customer compares Redwood billed tokens vs their local tokenizer and saw mismatches on tool-call heavy prompts.
- Requested: simple explanation in writing: billed_tokens vs raw tokens, tool-call attribution, and whether cache hits are billable.
- Action: share the updated usage FAQ excerpt + provide a one-page explanation customized for Acme’s integration.

Technical / product requirements surfaced
- Usage reporting: needs daily usage export aligned to America/Los_Angeles business day; wants separate columns for prompt/completion/billed_tokens and cache_hit attribution.
- Idempotency: they will add idempotency keys client-side for retry safety; asked whether Redwood dedupes by request_id.
- Performance: prefers stable latency over absolute lowest cost for their production route; open to Dedicated if it offers steadier throughput.

Stakeholders (customer)
- Primary: Director of Engineering (platform), FinOps lead, and Procurement counsel.
- Champion: Platform Eng manager running the LLM gateway.

Internal owners / coordination
- AE/Owner: Jordan Blake (pulled in due to renewal + dispute sensitivity).
- SE: Monica Patel (SDK + retries/idempotency alignment).
- Finance: Emily Stone coordinating RevRec-safe remediation path; Rishi Malhotra watching margin impact.
- Eng: Logan Wright / Nadia Rahman for meter attribution; Connor O’Brien for shadow-billing rollout coordination.

Next steps (2 weeks)
- Provide Acme a "Feb usage reconciliation pack":
  - ledger totals vs usage-event aggregates vs customer usage export
  - explanation of billed_tokens vs raw tokens; include streaming + tool-call notes
  - call out known incident window(s) and whether they affected billing
- Confirm whether we will issue a credit for any verified duplicate metering (requires Finance + Legal sign-off).
- Renewal order form: incorporate updated usage definition language (retries/fallback, caching attribution, dispute window). Schedule procurement review once language is ready.

Links / references (internal)
- Slack: finance/1770043123-usage-recon-discrepancy-acme
- Fireflies: sales-calls/2026-02-06-pricing-and-usage-review-with-acme-ai
- Gmail: laura_bennett/20260205-metering-discrepancy-and-revrec-impact
- Jira: SUP-28451, SUP-28463

title: Sable Harbor Financial Tech
preview:
Account background: midwestern regional fintech offering B2B payment rails and treasury services. Early AI usage for customer support and transaction enrichment. Very conservative security posture — must pass internal infosec and legal before procurement.

Encryption posture (customer focus):
- In-transit: require mTLS for all control-plane and data-plane connections. Mandate mutual certs for any Redwood endpoint; no plain TLS only allowed. Use of client cert rotation every 30 days preferred.
- At-rest: AES-256-GCM envelope encryption across database, object storage, and ephemeral caches. Customer requires proof of encryption-at-rest for token caches and model KV caches.
- KMS integration: BYOK required for production keys. Customer prefers AWS KMS with HSM-backed key material (CloudHSM) or external HSM via AWS External Key Store. Key rotation policy: automatic rotation with 90-day rotation window, ability to force immediate rotation via API.
- Secrets management: integrate with HashiCorp Vault (dynamic secrets) or AWS Secrets Manager; prefer Vault for on-prem and VPC deployments. Must support short-lived DB credentials and rotated TLS client certs.
- Key access controls: IAM mapping & least privilege; separation of duties between ops and application accounts; audit trails for every KMS decrypt request.
- Audit logging: All request/response metadata (headers, timestamps, user identifiers) to be forwarded to customer Splunk instance over secure forwarder or stored in S3 with SSE and cross-account access. Must include request-id correlation for traces. Retention: default 7 years for audit logs; shorter retention for any PII — redaction policy required.
- Data residency: primary data must remain in NA (us-east-1) but some EU customer data requires regional pinning. Customer expects regional isolation per tenant and writable audit trails in-region.
- KV cache / telemetry: customer insists on control over retention of KV cache and telemetry. Want option to disable persistent KV caching in private deployment or set TTL=0 for sensitive workloads.

Security review history (high level):
- 2025-11-20: initial inbound from solutions team, MQL->AE.
- 2025-12-02: discovery call (owner Maya) – high-level requirements collected.
- 2025-12-15: Sable sent Redwood security questionnaire (150+ Qs).
- 2026-01-12: technical intro meeting w/ infra + security (Fireflies ff_2026-01-12_sable-intro). Key ask: BYOK + HSM, mTLS, audit retention.
- 2026-02-05: provided SOC2 & ISO27001 certs links and network diagrams (drive link).
- 2026-02-22: KMS demo + PoC kickoff (Fireflies ff_2026-02-22_kms-demo). Deployed private VPC staging; integrated with customer's Vault test instance.
- 2026-02-25: security team provided list of missing items: pen-test, encryption key policy templates, architecture diagram showing PrivateLink/VPC endpoints.

POC notes:
- Staging VPC provisioned; connection via AWS PrivateLink and transit gateway for restricted peering.
- Proof-of-concept integrated with customer's Vault for secrets and AWS KMS for envelope keys; demonstrated decrypt audit events forwarded to customer's Splunk.
- mTLS client certs were tested using short-lived certs; challenge: automating cert rotation end-to-end (we need script + runbook).

Customer quotes / paraphrases: "We cannot accept any unmanaged plaintext logs — must have deterministic redaction and clear retention rules." — Security lead.

Open technical questions for SE team:
- Provide KMS key policy templates for BYOK + cross-account decrypt.
- Confirm support for CloudHSM-backed CMKs or EKMS.
- Details on how Redwood encrypts ephemeral KV cache and whether it can use customer-supplied keys for that layer.
- Runbook for cert rotation and orchestration for mTLS client certs.

Next steps (short):
- Deliver architecture diagram showing Private VPC layout, PrivateLink endpoints, and KMS flow (by 2026-03-05).
- Secure legal/procurement confirmation for BYOK in SOW.
- Schedule 60m KMS deep-dive with Ethan + customer infra (target week of 2026-03-08).

Sales notes / messaging:
- Highlight: Redwood Private supports VPC deployments with KMS integration and audit log forwarding — emphasize envelope encryption and HSM support.
- Cost sensitivity: medium — willing to pay premium for HSM/BYOK and extended retention.
- Close plan: finalize SOW for dedicated slot + Private deployment; include SOC2/ISO certificates and third-party pen-test results.

Misc: Jira tickets opened: INFRA-2345 (KMS BYOK support), SEC-7621 (log retention policy).

title: Cerulean ML Enterprises
preview:
- 2024-11-02: Intro call with CTO Alex Martinez and Head of ML Lina Park. Very interested in latency and cost math.
- 2024-11-20: Demo (streaming + embeddings). AE Jordan did walkthrough; flagged prefetching + KV cache as must-haves.
- 2024-12-05: Security deep dive with Cerulean SecOps (Fireflies FF-20241205-cerulean-security-deepdive). Primary ask: bring-your-own-KMS, audited key rotation, and on-prem option for PII workloads.

title: Stratagem Vaulting Systems
preview:
Summary: Large European fintech evaluating Redwood Private (VPC + optional on-prem) for customer-facing support assistants and multi-tenant embeddings index. Primary drivers: strict EU data residency, vendor-managed private control plane, demonstrable SOC 2/ISO controls, and customer-managed keys.

Recent cadence:
- 2025-10-05: Initial inbound (RFP) — account created, basic requirements captured.
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (Jordan + Priya) — high-level architecture discussion; client wants dedicated VPC per region.
- 2025-12-08: Security questionnaire submitted; client asked for SOC 2 Type II report and ISO scope.
- 2026-01-12: Fireflies call (ff_20260112_7b9c2e) — walked through KMS options, audit log forwarding, and retention policy.
- 2026-01-28: Follow-up RFC deck shared (/drive/pricing/stratagem_vaulting_private_deploy_v1.pptx).
- 2026-02-20: SE deep-dive — discussed envelope encryption, HSM support, and PCI scoping for limited payment metadata.

Compliance mapping (high level):
- SOC 2: customer requires Type II report covering CC6 (change management), CC7 (system operations), CC8 (logical and physical access). They want evidence of audit logging, role-based access control, and quarterly vulnerability scans. Key ask: how Redwood segregates logs for private VPCs and access to audit artifacts.
- ISO 27001: need ISMS scope that includes private deploy control plane, risk assessment artifacts, and continuous monitoring/incident response procedures. Client expects ability to map Redwood controls to ISO annex A controls.
- HIPAA / BAA: some business lines process PHI (claims notes) during support interactions. Legal insists on a signed BAA for any PHI processing. Also need encryption at rest + in transit, access logs, and support for record-of-access reporting.
- PCI considerations: client does NOT plan to store PAN in model payloads, but there is potential PII in support transcripts that may reference payment info. They need guidance on scoping Redwood's environment for PCI SAQ eligibility (they prefer Redwood to be out of scope where possible).

Technical requirements & asks:
- VPC: dedicated VPC per customer region, private subnets for inference nodes, customer-controlled egress, and strict peering rules.
- Control plane: private control plane option; customer prefers customer-managed control plane in EU (possible hybrid).
- KMS: CMK integration with AWS KMS and BYOK flow; HSM-backed keys for higher assurance and key rotation policy support. Envelope encryption for model weights and cache.
- Audit logging: CloudTrail-style logs, exported to customer SIEM (S3 + Kinesis); retention 12 months baseline, option for 7+ years. Immutable logs and tamper-evidence required for some business units.
- Data residency: all inference and caches must remain in eu-central-1; no fallback to other regions without explicit routing policy and approval.
- Authentication: SSO/SAML, SCIM for user provisioning, and RBAC for route/model access.
- Network isolation: option for air-gapped POCs for high-security lanes.

Client quotes / paraphrases from calls:
- "We need vendor controls we can map back to ISO and SOC artifacts — without that procurement won't approve."
- "BAA is non-negotiable for business lines touching PHI."
- "Show me proof we can manage keys and that logs are ours to retain and analyze."

POC / Technical validation plan (requested):
1) Security workshop with infra + legal (scheduled w/c 2026-03-15).
2) Short POC: private VPC in eu-central-1, CMK demo with rotation, and log forwarding to their SIEM (SIEM endpoint provided).
3) Deliver artifacts: SOC 2 Type II redacted, ISO annex mapping, draft BAA + DPA, PCI scoping note.

Risks & notes for AE/SE:
- Procurement window: client aligns vendor contract approvals to fiscal Q2 (close target 2026-04).
- Legal will push back on any vendor-managed shared-key models — emphasize CMK/BYOK path.
- Engineering ask: need architecture runbook for private control plane and what minimum telemetry Redwood requires to operate SLA.

Next actionable items (short):
- Provide redacted SOC 2 Type II and ISO scope docs (owner: Jordan) — due 2026-03-05.
- SE to prepare KMS/BYOK demo plan (owner: Priya) — due 2026-03-10.
- Schedule legal-to-legal call to discuss BAA terms (owner: Marcus) — target 2026-03-12.

title: Timberline SupplyChain Analytics
preview:
Account snapshot: pilot originally scoped Nov 2025 for reranking + embeddings across inventory signals. Strong technical fit: prefers Dedicated for predictable throughput and VPC for data residency. Key stakeholders: CTO (Marta Li), Head of ML (Daniel Osei), Dir. Procurement (R. Campos).

Recent timeline:
- 2025-11-20: Intro call (Jordan/SE). High-level product fit; agreed to tech deep-dive.
- 2025-12-03: Technical demo (Priya). Discussed batching, KV cache, quantization. Follow-up asked for perf numbers at 50 req/s.
- 2026-01-11: POC kickoff call (Fireflies ff_2026-01-11_8h4g2k). Agreed to 4-week POC: reranking on order-matching workload.
- 2026-01-25: POC progress sync. Initial throughput OK, cost estimate higher than expected.
- 2026-02-15: Procurement/legal requested SOC2 + KMS attestation; provided security FAQ (drive link).

Customer comments / quotes:
- "This is excellent technically, but we can't staff the integrations right now." — Head of ML (paraphrase)
- "Procurement put all new vendor spend on hold until headcount decisions are final." — Dir. Procurement

Current situation: POC incomplete (midway through evaluation). No formal purchase order. Stakeholder enthusiasm remains but no budget or integration team to continue work. Security asks were answered (SOC2 references, KMS checklist), but procurement prioritized freeze over contract negotiation.

Risks & win-back signals:
- Risk: Opportunity will drift to competitor if freeze lifts and they run a quicker procurement process.
- Signal to re-engage: HR/headcount memo clears; procurement allows pilot spend <= $25k.

Suggested cadence / playbook:
- Put account into "paused — revisit" workflow. Set reminder for 2026-06-01 for Exec hiring review follow-up.
- Prep a compact executive one-pager quantifying 6-month TCO reduction with Dedicated + Optimize (targeted to procurement).
- Offer a low-cost, time-boxed POC extension (60% scope) capped at $20k to keep momentum if procurement allows.

Next concrete steps (short):
- 2026-06-02: Jordan to email Marta and R. Campos with one-pager and ask for outcome of hiring review.
- If procurement re-opens, offer a compressed technical redelivery (Priya) to reduce integration time.

Internal notes (AE/SE):
- SE: POC artifacts stored in /drive/Timberline/POC-2026; can produce perf graphs for 8k-token flows on NVLink cluster.
- AE: Keep as warm lead; deprioritize pipeline score until June but do not archive.

Stalled reason (CRM tag): budget/hiring freeze — project paused indefinitely.

title: Northbridge Analytics
preview:
Primary requirement is production-safe optimization configuration changes under Redwood Optimize (batching/caching/quantization profiles). Northbridge wants config versioning, staged rollout w/ canary, and rollback that is fast and predictable. They consider this a 'change management' control (not just a product feature) and want it documented and contractable.

Key asks from Northbridge:
- Safe apply workflow: validate config + staged rollout (by tenant/model/region) w/ optional canary percentage.
- Gating: must pause/fail rollout on elevated 5xx and p95/p99 latency; for quantization profiles, must also gate on eval/quality signals (they asked how noisy evals are handled).
- Rollback: one-click rollback in UI and API; rollback should revert active config pointer to a prior immutable version; needs clear behavior if rollout is partially applied (what does 'rolled back' mean across workers).
- Audit logging: events for create/validate/apply/promote/rollback with actor identity, timestamp, scope (tenant/model/region), previous_version/new_version, request_id/correlation_id. Needs SIEM-friendly format + export story. Retention requested: 12 months minimum; ability to filter by tenant + model.
- Observability: want to see active config_version in dashboards and ideally in response headers for debugging ('when did the config change relative to the incident?').

Deployment/security constraints:
- Prefers Dedicated capacity in a VPC-style isolated deployment; legal language may refer to 'private networking' and customer-managed egress rules.
- SSO/SAML required for Console. KMS for secrets/key material. Data residency: US-only preferred for initial rollout.

Performance targets (POC notes):
- They care about tail latency stability more than raw median. Mentioned internal SLO: p95 < 900ms on chat for typical prompts; strong sensitivity to latency regressions during business hours.
- Cost sensitivity: moderate-high; they are explicitly experimenting with quantization profiles to reduce unit cost, but only with 'safety rails'.

title: Saltmarsh Insights Labs
preview:
Opportunity status: Closed-lost to OpenAI (direct API).

Summary:
- Vertical: fintech / portfolio analytics. Building conversational assistant + legal/regulatory doc search for portfolio managers.
- Initially interested in Redwood because of dedicated capacity + VPC option and model routing (important for compliance).
- Primary drivers: latency targets ~ <150ms for chat median, embedding batch throughput ~200 reqs/s for nightly rerank, strong audit logs and KMS.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2025-10-15: initial inbound product demo request (website sign-up). AE assigned (Maya).
- 2025-11-02: discovery call — compliance team on call; detailed questions on data residency and KMS. (ff-2026-01-12-8342 linked)
- 2025-12-07: POC kickoff — evaluated hosted_api + dedicated sizing. Engineering provided sample latency traces.
- 2026-01-12: Security deep-dive w/ security PM and infra (Fireflies meeting). Red flags: wanted audited HSM path + SLA for deletion/retention.
- 2026-01-30: Pricing negotiation — asked for 12 month committed discount. Presented pricing deck (drive link).
- 2026-02-10: Final exec sync; internal champion (Lead PM) informed AE they were pausing procurement due to finance head freeze and vendor preference for direct OpenAI/Anthropic APIs. (ff-2026-02-10-9011)
- 2026-02-18: Last touchpoint; formal close-lost communicated by procurement team.

Why we lost:
- Simplicity & brand: Procurement/product team cited "OpenAI brand and simple API" as lower friction for dev teams and faster time-to-production.
- Internal politics: Lead PM who championed Redwood moved teams in Jan — no replacement with same urgency.
- Budget freeze: Finance paused new vendor commitments while Q1 vendor consolidation occurs.
- Security nuance mismatch: They wanted explicit on-prem HSM details and audited deletion workflows; perceived Redwood's private/VPC answers as more complex than a direct API route.

What they liked about Redwood:
- Dedicated capacity concept and routing fallback options.
- Observability and rollout features fit their ops model.
- Detailed pricing and cost modeling in deck.

Open actions / follow-up hints:
- Procurement noted potential to re-evaluate if we publish a short whitepaper on HSM/KMS integrations and residency guarantees. (ask Product for 1-page security brief).
- Opportunity to re-engage once finance freezes lift or if the new PM re-prioritizes.
- Keep on competitor watch; they adopted OpenAI now but may need dedicated/private options as usage increases.

Stale items / internal notes:
- RWD-Sales: mark closed-lost, reason: vendor_preference_openai, no_current_champion, budget_hold.
- Suggest: target marketing playbook for branding vs direct-API objections; short security cheat-sheet for sale motions into fintech.

Quoted from procurement lead: "We love Redwood's depth but our teams want the quickest path to production — OpenAI wins on friction and brand familiarity."

title: Lexicor Compliance AI
preview:
Summary: Evaluation stalled -> closed-lost. Primary reason: quality regression on structured outputs and unreliable tool-calling vs incumbent (Anthropic). Redwood had clear ops & cost advantages but the customer prioritized deterministic JSON + tool reliability for downstream parsers.

Timeline / key interactions:
- 2025-10-02: Inbound demo request (legal ops team). Maya initial qual call. Use-case: extract clause-level obligations + produce canonical JSON for downstream risk engine.
- 2025-11-08: SE deep-dive with Ethan. Shared sample contracts, discussed schema constraints, residency and KMS.
- 2025-12-11: POC kickoff (3-week). Fireflies: ff_2025-12-11_lexicor_product-demo.
- 2025-12-20: POC notes: Redwood Dedicated throughput & token cost looked good. Early runs showed ~12% cost savings vs incumbent when batching enabled.
- 2026-01-05: POC QA: 3/4 runs had malformed JSON (missing keys), one run produced incorrect tool-call (asked retrieval tool for wrong doc id). Customer: "We can't accept occasional schema drift — risk engine breaks."
- 2026-01-18: Security review flagged timeline risk on KMS integration + E2E audit logs; Legal wanted on-prem proof-of-concept for residency.
- 2026-02-02: Pricing negotiation paused — internal budget freeze after Q4 results.
- 2026-02-15: Final evaluation meeting. Lexicor chose Anthropic Enterprise citing consistent structured outputs and a longer track record on tool-calling reliability.
- 2026-02-18: Account marked closed-lost. Maya logged lessons learned.

Customer quotes / sentiments:
- "Great throughput numbers, but our parsers need predictable JSON every time."
- "We saw a regression in tool-calling ties vs what the other vendor delivered — that was a deal breaker."

Technical takeaways for Redwood:
- Structured-output guardrails need tightening: stricter schema enforcement and post-generation validators in the serving path (fail-open vs fail-closed policy discussion).
- Tool-calling reliability: reproduce failure conditions from POC (prompting + streaming edge cases) and add resiliency tests.
- Security onboarding timeline was longer than they expected; make KMS integration playbook more prescriptive.

Next steps / reopen conditions:
- No active champion (legal ops lead left in Jan) — low chance to reopen in next 3 months.

Summary: Evaluation stalled -> closed-lost. Primary reason: quality regression on structured outputs and unreliable tool-calling vs incumbent (Anthropic). Redwood had clear ops & cost advantages but the customer prioritized deterministic JSON + tool reliability for downstream parsers.

Timeline / key interactions:
- 2025-10-02: Inbound demo request (legal ops team). Maya initial qual call. Use-case: extract clause-level obligations + produce canonical JSON for downstream risk engine.
- 2025-11-08: SE deep-dive with Ethan. Shared sample contracts, discussed schema constraints, residency and KMS.
- 2025-12-11: POC kickoff (3-week). Fireflies: ff_2025-12-11_lexicor_product-demo.
- 2025-12-20: POC notes: Redwood Dedicated throughput & token cost looked good. Early runs showed ~12% cost savings vs incumbent when batching enabled.
- 2026-01-05: POC QA: 3/4 runs had malformed JSON (missing keys), one run produced incorrect tool-call (asked retrieval tool for wrong doc id). Customer: "We can't accept occasional schema drift — risk engine breaks."
- 2026-01-18: Security review flagged timeline risk on KMS integration + E2E audit logs; Legal wanted on-prem proof-of-concept for residency.
- 2026-02-02: Pricing negotiation paused — internal budget freeze after Q4 results.
- 2026-02-15: Final evaluation meeting. Lexicor chose Anthropic Enterprise citing consistent structured outputs and a longer track record on tool-calling reliability.
- 2026-02-18: Account marked closed-lost. Maya logged lessons learned.

Customer quotes / sentiments:
- "Great throughput numbers, but our parsers need predictable JSON every time."
- "We saw a regression in tool-calling ties vs what the other vendor delivered — that was a deal breaker."

Technical takeaways for Redwood:
- Structured-output guardrails need tightening: stricter schema enforcement and post-generation validators in the serving path (fail-open vs fail-closed policy discussion).
- Tool-calling reliability: reproduce failure conditions from POC (prompting + streaming edge cases) and add resiliency tests.
- Security onboarding timeline was longer than they expected; make KMS integration playbook more prescriptive.

Next steps / reopen conditions:
- No active champion (legal ops lead left in Jan) — low chance to reopen in next 3 months.
- Recommended re-engage cadence: 6 months with demo of structured-output improvements + formal KMS integration timeline.
- If model/tool-calling regressions are fixed and we can provide a hardened JSON schema guarantee, request a reconsideration.

title: Acme Health
preview:
Primary workload: (1) clinician-facing summarization of long-form notes (streaming preferred), (2) internal doc-search/RAG over policies/procedures, (3) support-agent style Q&A for staff. Throughput: initial ~50–150 concurrent users, spiky during business hours; latency goal stated as “<2s time-to-first-token for typical prompts” for the summarization UI. Strong cost sensitivity for long-context summaries; asked about batching/caching and model/quantization options.

Deployment preference: Private (VPC) strongly preferred; Dedicated is a fallback only if we can document strict isolation, retention controls, and clear shared responsibility. They explicitly do not want prompts/responses retained beyond what is necessary for operations.

Security/compliance: SOC 2 Type II report required under NDA. Asked multiple times about HIPAA/BAA: want a vendor willing to sign a BAA for any workflow that could include PHI. SSO is required (Okta); SCIM requested for user lifecycle. RBAC: need separation between platform admins (security team), billing admins, and app developers. Audit logging: must cover admin actions + auth events; export to SIEM. Encryption: in transit TLS; at rest encryption with preference for customer-managed keys (KMS) in Private. Retention/deletion: need clear default retention statement and ability to configure log retention; also asked for deletion SLA for transient request data.

title: Sterling Citadel Systems
preview:
Topline: large defense-tier contact center integrator. Onboarding for mission-critical 24/7 ops. Must be fully air-gapped for classification boundary; cannot allow outbound tokens.

Summary of requirements:
- Workloads: live agent assist (real-time cues), post-call ticket summarization (structured), tool-calling to case mgmt and escalation workflows, and automated surge handling during incidents.
- Perf targets: median agent assist latency <120ms for token stream (per-agent), sustained throughput 10k tokens/sec during peak events, burst to 50k tokens/sec for incident windows.
- Reliability: automatic failover to local fallback models when primary unavailable; prefer deterministic degrade to text-only (no remote fallthrough).
- Models: open-model preference for on-prem licensing; need support for quantized LLMs and ability to pin to specific model versions.
- Security: FIPS-validated crypto, HSM integration for key storage, STIG-hardened images, full audit logging with 7+ years retention for certain records, role-based redaction pipeline for PII.

Recent meeting notes (shorthand):
- 2026-01-22: Discovery call (Fireflies ff_2026-01-22_sterling-arch-review). AE Jordan + SE Priya. They sketched air-gap architecture. Customer: "no egress, no exceptions".
- 2026-01-29: Deep-dive with infra/security team. Provided security FAQ (drive link). Customer asked for STIG checklist and vendor attestation sample.
- 2026-02-03: Proof-of-concept scoping call. Agreed POC to run in their SOC2 lab for 3 weeks, STIGed VM images, and local-only model bundle. SE to deliver POC plan by 2026-02-10.
- 2026-02-05: Pricing/contract framing; procurement asked for 3 customer references with on-prem CCaaS integration.

Tech considerations / tradeoffs called out:
- Tool-calling: need secure connectors to Genesys/Avaya on-prem APIs; connector must be packaged as appliance - no outbound webhook to Redwood cloud.
- KV/prefix caching must be local-only; any caching telemetry must be scrubbed or kept offline.
- Observability: they want token-level cost breakdown but only via on-prem console; cannot ship raw traces offsite.
- Quantization: recommend 8-bit LLAMA variants; customer wants Perplexity/QA results run on their eval suite.

POC acceptance criteria (customer-specified):
- On-prem bundle deployed into isolated lab with no internet.
- Real-time assist latency under 180ms 95th pct during 1k concurrent agents.
- Tool-calling workflows executed end-to-end with audit logs captured locally.
- Ability to roll back model version with <5 minute downtime.

Next steps (short):
- SE (Priya) to deliver detailed POC runbook and STIG checklist by 2026-02-10.
- Legal to provide draft MSA with indemnities for air-gapped deployment.
- Customer to supply procurement RFQ and three reference contacts.

Quoted lines/quotes: "If you can't run inside our enclave, it won't pass procurement." - Director of Support.

CRM extras: Gmail thread gm_thread_Ab12cdEfGhIJ has pricing discussion; JIRA-CC-4021 opened for connector requirements.

Short-term risks: procurement timeline (Q2 2026), FedRAMP expectation may push timeline out, reference requirement could delay award.

Contact cadence: weekly on Wed 10am PT until POC starts.

preview:

2025-11-05 ff meeting id ff_2025-11-05_amd123 — transcript: 'we can't introduce extra billing silos, needs to show up on GCP bill' (VP infra)

2025-12-01 — Security questionnaire submitted; flagged key rotation SLA, audit log retention, and data residency for EU traffic. Security asked for SOC2 + ISO27001 evidence.

2026-01-26 — Pricing review: Redwood TCO competitive at scale but hyperscaler offered an internal discount + consolidated billing; procurement favored single-vendor billing.

2026-01-28 ff meeting id ff_2026-01-12_pq9b — Exec call: Decision leaning to GCP Vertex AI for ops simplicity. Quote: 'we have a six-figure commitment to GCP next quarter; difficult to justify another hosting contract'.

title: NorthPoint Risk Technologies
preview:
Status: Closed-lost (chose Fireworks for on-prem inference pilot + Replicate for quick dev endpoints). Reasons layered: security HSM proof, faster on-prem turnkey, internal budget hold, champion turnover.

Timeline (CRM bullets):
- 2025-08-20: Lead created (web form). AE assigned: Ethan. Initial interest: low-latency fraud chat + reranking for alerts.
- 2025-09-10: Discovery call w/ CTO (Liam O.) + Head of Fraud (Sasha). Asked about VPC/private deployment and KMS/HSM options.
- 2025-10-02: NDA + SOW request. POC scope: 10M tokens/month, p50 latency target 30ms, sustained 75 qps.
- 2025-11-19: Demo + live POC kickoff (Fireflies ff_2025-11-19-northpoint-poc-demo). Setup uncovered network constraints for on-prem pilot.
- 2025-12-07: Security questionnaire (70 Qs) — main open items: proof of HSM integration, explicit data residency SLA, and audit log retention controls.
- 2026-01-12: Procurement flags  hiring/budget pause  central infra review required for any vendor committing to on-prem tools.
- 2026-01-27: Final procurement decision: split-vendor approach. Fireworks for on-prem trial (promised HSM proof-of-concept + engineer embedded support); Replicate for dev endpoints to reduce dev friction. Redwood marked closed-lost.

Call notes / quotes: 
- "The security team needs to see a reproducible HSM handshake and local key control before we can sign anything major."  Head of Security (email 2025-12-10).
- "We appreciated Redwood's batching and cost recommendations but the on-prem demo timeline was longer than Fireworks could promise." 	AE note 2026-01-20.

Technical summary: 
- Primary workloads: interactive fraud analyst chat + automated reranking for transaction scoring; embeddings for alert search.
- SLOs: chat p50 20-40ms, p95 <120ms; baseline 75 qps, peak 300 qps bursts during market open.
- Cost stance: cost-aware, willing to commit to reserved capacity if security + residency checkboxes met.
- Model preferences: open models with quantization + fast KV-cache; fallbacks to smaller models for non-critical paths.
- Ops needs: canary rollouts, per-request audit headers, retention policies per region.

Why we lost (brief):
- Security: Fireworks committed to on-prem HSM PoC and local key management faster than Redwood's delivery estimate.
- Procurement: hiring/budget freeze delayed appetite for committed Dedicated spend; preferred phased vendor approach.
- Champion churn: original product sponsor moved teams mid-POC; successor deprioritized immediate rollout.
- Speed/Time-to-eval: Replicate offered rapid self-serve endpoints that satisfied dev teams for short-term needs.

Immediate next steps (internal):
- Update CRM: mark closed-lost, competitor tags: Fireworks, Replicate.
- Save redacted security Q&A to /drive/security/northpoint/ for future RFP leverage.
- Close POC tickets: INF-9017 (performance tuning), SEC-1310 (KMS follow-up).
- Set reminder: re-open in 10 months (2026-11) to check if infra budget/comply mandates changed.

Stubs/links: 
- Pricing deck: /drive/pricing_decks/northpoint/redwood_enterprise_quote_2025.pdf
- Security Q&A: /drive/security/northpoint/sec_q_and_a_2025.xlsx
- Fireflies demo: ff_2025-11-19-northpoint-poc-demo
- Jira: INF-9017, SEC-1310

AE shorthand / outlook:
- Lost but not burned. High re-engage potential if their compliance deadlines tighten or HSM proof becomes mandatory.
- Maintain contact list: CTO Liam O. (liam.oliver@northpoint-risk.com), Head of Fraud Sasha M. (sasha.m@northpoint-risk.com).
- Recommend short quarterly check-ins and share new HSM/KMS playbook once available.

title: Haven Arc Trust Systems
preview:
Summary: Large defense-adjacent contact center operator evaluating Redwood Private for agent-assist and ticket summarization in a fully air-gapped environment. Primary concern is deterministic, auditable outputs and resilience during surge events.

Key requirements: minimal dependency on outbound network, ability to run in air-gapped on-prem datacenter, FIPS/HSM KMS support, audit log continuity, SSO via enterprise SAML, retention controls per DoD guidelines. Integration must sit in front of existing Genesys/Avaya ACD and call-routing stack; need tool-calling to surface backend actions (ticket creation, CRM lookups) without exposing external endpoints.

Performance targets discussed on 2026-01-15 call: 600-900 concurrent agents at peak; target 1st-token latency <250ms for agent-assist in most flows; summary generation SLA <500ms average; deterministic reranking for KB hits with explainability traces. Cost sensitivity moderate — prefer reserved capacity for predictable ARR but need private deployment pricing.

POC scope proposed: 6-week on-prem pilot in isolated DC (air-gapped): ingest 2M recent tickets for summarization evals, deploy one model variant quantized for CPU fallback, test spike generator for 2x expected peak traffic and run chaos tests. Success metrics: 90%+ summary usefulness in blind evals, <2% critical divergence from human labelers, no external egress during run.

Stakeholder quotes/highlights: “Can’t have any egress without explicit approval — that’s non-starter.” — Head of Infrastructure. “We need an auditable chain for every suggestion the agent gets.” — Contact Center Ops Lead.

Integration notes: will require custom installer for offline RPMs/containers, support for custom CA bundles, per-tenant model pinning, and policy-driven fallback (dedicated->local CPU). Red team will run a penetration and compliance validation week.

Commercial: procurement prefers 12–24 month committed capacity; ask for SOW draft and sample security addendum. Pricing deck shared (drive link).

Open technical risks: HSM integration timeline (vendor delay), on-prem GPU resource provisioning for quantized LLMs, telephony tool-calling latency under NAT/firewall rules.

Short next steps: SOW draft (Redwood) due 2026-01-30; schedule HSM scoping with customer vendor 2026-02-05; security walkthrough with network + red team week of 2026-02-12.

title: Lumen Legaltech
preview:
Account summary
- Compliance-heavy (legal/regulatory). Building LLM features into internal case management + customer-facing contract review workflows.
- Strong preference for Dedicated capacity (predictable throughput + isolation). Private/VPC deployment likely required for production.

Firmographics / environment
- HQ: US (East). Primary users: enterprise legal ops + compliance teams.
- Current infra: AWS (us-east-1), strict network egress controls. Mentioned potential future EU footprint (data residency questions).

What they care about (high signal)
1) Auditability of scaling policy changes (hard requirement)
- They explicitly asked how we prove and reconstruct: who changed autoscaling config, when, what was changed (before/after), and which deployment(s) were affected.
- Quote (security review, 2025-07-22): “We need an immutable trail of any capacity or scaling change because it can materially impact user access and cost.”
- They want audit events not only for human-initiated updates (Console/API/Terraform), but also for autoscaler decisions (scale up/down) with reasoning.

2) Change management + RBAC
- Need role-based access controls for scaling policy updates (min/max bounds, predictive toggle, cooldowns).
- Asked whether we can restrict changes to a small admin group and whether reads can be broader (auditors).
- Asked about approval workflows; we do not have built-in approvals, but they can use internal change tickets + our audit logs as evidence.

3) Retention + export
- Retention request: 12 months minimum for audit logs; preferred 18–24 months.
- Export: they use Splunk and asked for either webhook streaming or periodic export (daily ok if guaranteed delivery + integrity).
- Also asked if we can provide a “config diff” view for audits.

4) Dedicated scaling bounds feature alignment
- They like the concept of customer-set min/max bounds as a spend control + baseline SLO guarantee.
- Concern: “If the platform changes behavior silently, we’ll fail audits.” They want explicit enablement, clearly documented semantics, and per-deployment effective policy visibility.

Workload details (current / planned)
- Use case: contract summarization + clause extraction, some chat-style assistant for internal users.
- Usage pattern: business hours heavy, some end-of-month spikes. Wants predictable p95 latency during peak.
- Model stance: open to Redwood-recommended models; more sensitive to compliance + audit trails than specific model brand.

Timeline of recent activity
- 2025-06-26: Inbound via partner referral; initial discovery (Dedicated vs Hosted).
- 2025-07-10: Technical deep dive on Dedicated capacity + VPC networking.
- 2025-07-22: Security review focused on audit logging + config change controls (Fireflies linked).
- 2025-08-05: Procurement call; MSA redlines returned; security appendix flagged audit logging and retention as required.
- 2025-08-07: Internal alignment with Platform team on upcoming autoscaling bounds + scaling decision audit events; created INT-9588 to track customer-facing answers + timeline.

Open questions to answer back to customer
- Exact audit event fields we can commit to for: scaling policy changes (min/max, predictive mode, cooldowns) and autoscaler decisions (requested desired vs bounded desired + reason).
- Retention guarantee by tier for enterprise Dedicated + whether export to SIEM is supported (or roadmap + interim solution).
- Clarify how “effective policy” is displayed (Console/API) and whether we can provide periodic evidence reports.

Internal notes / strategy
- This account is a strong fit for the autoscaling policy auditability workstream. If we can close the audit log + retention/export gap, likelihood to close is high.
- Coordinate responses with: Marcus Lin (security review), Kevin Osei (audit log export/SIEM), Felix Schneider (Console audit/scaling views).
- Avoid overpromising approvals workflow; emphasize deterministic audit trail + RBAC + integration to their existing change management.

Sentiment
- Positive on product performance and Dedicated isolation.
- Primary risk is compliance sign-off: they will not proceed without auditable scaling/config changes documented in writing.

title: Lattice Search
preview:
Account summary
- Enterprise doc-search team building an internal + customer-facing knowledge search experience across tickets, docs, and Slack/Confluence exports.
- Strong preference for a "production launch checklist" they can use as an internal approval artifact (security + platform teams want a clear list of go-live requirements).

Workload / technical fit
- Primary workload: embeddings + reranking for document retrieval; secondary: summarization of retrieved passages.
- Latency: wants p95 < 500ms for rerank on typical query traffic; embeddings batch jobs can be async.
- Model posture: open to Redwood catalog defaults, but wants ability to pin model version and have a defined rollback/fallback policy.
- Observability: wants token/cost breakdown + request tracing hooks; asked specifically how to audit "who changed model settings" and "when".

Security + compliance (main theme)
- Customer quote (paraphrased): "We can’t get this through internal review without a clear go-live checklist and evidence we followed it."
- Requirements called out repeatedly:
  - SSO/SAML for Console access and role-based access controls.
  - Audit logging (admin actions + model/config changes).
  - KMS integration for keys/secrets at rest (customer-managed keys preferred; at minimum, clear encryption statement).
  - Data retention controls for logs/telemetry; hard requirement to avoid PII in onboarding/progress events.
  - Data residency: mixed — US default ok, but EU subsidiaries require EU region storage and clarity on where telemetry is stored.

Procurement / timeline
- Customer-side procurement process is active; target to sign by end of April pending security questionnaire + DPA redlines.
- Gating items: security team questionnaire, legal DPA review, and platform team sign-off on VPC architecture.

Onboarding checklist / product feedback
- They responded well to the Console checklist concept, but want:
  - Exportable proof (screenshot/PDF or API) of checklist completion for internal change management.
  - Clear definitions of "First success" vs "First production traffic" and what events count.
  - Explicit steps around retries/timeouts, 429 handling, streaming disconnect behavior (even if their first phase is not streaming).
  - A step for quotas/budgets and alerting (they’ve been burned by surprise usage spikes).

Open questions
- Confirm preferred deployment path: Dedicated in Redwood-managed VPC vs Redwood Private in their VPC (leaning Private).
- Confirm regions required on day 1 (US only vs US+EU).
- Validate whether any documents include regulated data categories (HIPAA/PCI) — customer said "no" but needs verification.

Internal owners / handoffs
- Ben Carter (owner): coordinate procurement timeline + stakeholder map.
- Brian Murphy (SE): drive technical validation (routing/fallback, observability hooks, audit logging expectations).
- Dev Patel (Support/CS): review what support needs to interpret checklist state during pilot + escalation paths.

Next actions
- Send security packet: onboarding telemetry data review summary, event schema notes (PII avoidance), retention defaults + knobs.
- Provide VPC/private deployment architecture overview + responsibilities (shared responsibility model).
- Share draft "First Production Launch" checklist template and confirm which steps are mandatory for their internal approval.

title: Mariner Insights Platform
preview:
Account background:
- Customer since 2023 (started on hosted API for embeddings + reranking). Started POC for Dedicated in Q4-2024 based on seasonal throughput needs. Renewal for hosted contract due 2025-06 but want to fold into Dedicated reserved capacity SOW.

Primary drivers:
- Predictable throughput for peak chartering season (May-Oct).
- Strong need for regional residency (APAC) for some customers and partners.
- Cost predictability: looking to move from per-token hosted spend to committed Dedicated with Optimize to reduce unit costs.

Technical requirements / targets (from SE calls):
- Workload: real-time dispatch suggestions (chat-like), bulk document embeddings/search, post-voyage summarization (batch).
- Latency target: p95 < 300ms for short-turn dispatch queries; tail latency most important.
- Throughput: sustained ~120–250 RPS at peak across multimodel routes.
- Model preferences: open-models (Llama2-13B quantized) validated in POC; fallbacks to smaller 7B variants for cost-sensitive routes.
- Routing: need policy-based routing (latency tier + cost tier) and automatic fallback to compatible quantized variants when Dedicated pool saturated.
- Security: SSO/SAML, KMS-managed key rotation, SOC2 + ISO27001 required, full audit logs and 1yr+ retention for access logs, APAC residency for subset of customer data.

POC & benchmarks (recent):
- Jan 2025 POC run against quantized 13B on g4dn-like nodes. Results: avg latency 170ms for short prompts; p95 measured 410ms — flagged by SE.
- Optimize pilot (two-week) reduced token spend on reranking pipeline by ~28% using autosuggestion batching + prefix caching. They liked caching behavior.
- Canary run with Dedicated staging pool (Feb 2025) saw improved stability but observed p95 spikes when KV cache warmup occurred. SE investigating warmup heuristics.

Engagement timeline (highlights):
- 2024-11-07: Intro + renewal timeline (hosted contract) — owner Ava.
- 2024-12-12: SE workshop (Diego) — architecture review, discussed VPC and on-prem options.
- 2025-01-09: Fireflies ff_20250109_4421 — POC kickoff, data residency scoping.
- 2025-01-28: Security deep-dive — provided SOC2 artifacts; asked for ISO27001 attestation.
- 2025-02-12: POC results review (ff_20250212_7938) — Optimize savings & latency gap noted.
- 2025-02-20: Pricing review with finance + CSM (Nina) — redlines on SOW.

Quotes & call snippets:
- \"We need predictable throughput for May — outages there cost us charters.\" — Head of Ops.
- \"If p95 doesn't hit the target, we can't commit to the higher ARR band.\" — Procurement lead.

Risks / blockers (details):
- APAC region pricing model still being finalized; affects cost-to-serve calculation for multi-region SOW.
- Legal needs explicit data-residency clause for Singapore and Japan customers — currently flagged by legal team.
- Benchmark discrepancy: POC p95 > target (410ms vs target 300ms). SE action: run NVMe-backed cache config + kernel selection tuning.

Commercials & next steps:
- They prefer a quarterly ramp for Dedicated capacity: 50% commit first 3 months, full commit ahead of peak season. Want Optimize adoption as mandatory add-on to realize cost savings.
- Next step: finalize Dedicated machine types + autoscaling policy (Diego to provide config by 2025-03-05).
- Legal: Redwood legal to supply APAC addendum draft by 2025-03-10. Procurement to sign SOW once p95 tuning and APAC pricing confirmed.
- CSM (Nina) to run 30-day Optimize onboarding workshop post-sign.

Quick notes (CRM shorthand):
- Pricing deck v2 shared (drive link).
- Procurement asked for EOR clause; redlines in SOW (INF-3421).
- Security: sent KMS integration checklist; awaiting signed NDA for on-prem docs.
- Happy with Optimize pilot savings; major win for commercial.

Attachments / refs: drive://redwood/pricing/mariner-dedicated-quote-v2.pdf, drive://redwood/security/mariner-security-answers-2025.docx, drive://redwood/pocs/mariner-poc-results-jan2025.xlsx

title: CinderWorks AI
preview:

Primary objection = total run-rate (GPU + token) and size/duration of commit required to reach the quoted unit price. title: Fountwell Research
preview:
POC summary: building a RAG-powered knowledge portal for sell-side research and internal desk research. Primary pipeline: nightly ingestion of PDFs/earnings_transcripts + SEC filings -> paragraph-level passages -> embeddings store -> retrieve+rerank. Index size target: ~120M passages (50-70M docs collapsed to passages). Latency/throughput needs: interactive search for analysts (p95 < 120ms on rerank path), bursty workloads during open/close with sustained 400-600 QPS and peaks to 1k QPS. Cost sensitivity: procurement asked for unit economics — target $0.005-0.02 per query depending on one vs two-stage rerank. Security/residency: data must stay in NA region, customer-managed KMS keys required, SOC2 + audit logging mandatory. Preferred deployment: Dedicated pools in VPC; evaluating private/air-gapped on prem for regulated clients.

Key quotes: "We can't accept any residency leakage — keys must be customer-controlled." — Head of Data (K. Morales). "If p95 can't hit ~120ms for our 2-stage rerank, we need to consider simpler rerank or more capacity." — CTO.

POC approach discussed: 1) 10M sample index run on hosted API to validate similarity metrics (done), 2) 50M staging index on Dedicated (requested sizing), 3) full-scale 120M run for latency/cost verification. POC metrics to capture: embedding QPS, vector search latency, CPU/GPU utilization on Dedicated nodes, cost per query breakdown (embedding + ANN + rerank), index rebuild time, KV/cache hit rates.

Legal/Procurement notes: DPA + KMS SOW outstanding. Internal procurement requests line-item for reserved capacity (12-36 months) and breach SLA credits. Security requested HSM integration details and key rotation policy.

Sales/next touch: SE to deliver Dedicated sizing worksheet and cost-model by 2025-02-20. AE to schedule final security review w/ security engineer and legal on 2025-02-25. Procurement kick-off planned after SOW sign-off.

title: Oakridge Intelligence Group
preview:
Status: Closed - lost to hyperscaler managed endpoints (AWS).

Summary bullets:
- Late-stage procurement; heavy emphasis from procurement and infra on reducing vendor footprint.
- POC (embeddings + semantic search) ran on Redwood Dedicated in a single VPC subnet (POC cluster spun for 2 weeks). Latency and relevance metrics met internal SLOs.
- Cost model: Redwood estimated ~30-40% TCO advantage at forecasted scale (with caching + batching) but procurement prioritized single-cloud consolidation and integrated billing.
- Security review: Redwood passed technical answers; security team flagged support burden of a second inference vendor despite VPC option. Quote from SecOps: "we can't add another inference vendor into our stack right now — enterprise AWS contract covers managed endpoints and support." 
- Decision: Oakridge procurement selected AWS-managed endpoints (SageMaker/Bedrock) to keep vendor count down and to leverage existing enterprise discount and monitoring integrations.

Timeline (recent activity):
- 2024-11-12: Intro discovery call (Jordan B.) — high-level requirements, use-case fit (embeddings + underwriting chat).
- 2024-11-29: Technical deep dive with SE (Maya C.) — runtime/latency expectations, VPC connectivity discussed.
- 2024-12-08: POC kickoff — Redwood provided dedicated test pool and sample connectors into S3/RedShift.
- 2025-01-18: Security questionnaire submitted; follow-up Q&A with SecOps.
- 2025-01-22: POC wrap & demo (fireflies ff-2025-01-22-oi-poceval). Results: relevance + latency OK; cost projection disputed by procurement.
- 2025-02-06: Procurement decision meeting — confirmed preference for AWS-managed endpoints.
- 2025-02-10: Final email from procurement. "We will onboard with AWS endpoints to simplify ops and unify support. Open to revisit at renewal."

Why we lost (main drivers):
- Procurement policy requiring cloud-native / managed endpoints where possible to reduce vendor management overhead.
- Existing enterprise AWS contract + internal incentives (billing/observability/managed support) made hyperscaler option administratively simpler.
- No strong internal champion to fight consolidation decision — infra prioritized least operational friction.

Objections raised from Oakridge that were not fully resolved:
- Concern about multi-cloud vendor sprawl (their priority) vs. Redwood argument for cost/latency tradeoffs.
- Questions around single-signoff for incident response and vendor SLAs; they preferred a single hyperscaler support path.

Next actions for Redwood (internal):
- Mark account closed-lost; record hyperscaler as primary loss reason.
- Set reminder for 6-month renewal window and account health check (possible re-engage if AWS experience is poor or for non-core workloads).
- Add POA: create a short note bundle comparing Redwood Dedicated + VPC vs. AWS managed endpoints with clear ops and cost tradeoffs for future AE outreach.

Stubs/links: pricing deck in Drive, security questionnaire spreadsheet, fireflies transcript id included above.

title: Palisade Government Knowledge Systems
preview:
Summary: Palisade is a homeland-focused contractor building an enterprise search product for state and federal agencies. Primary ask: high-quality RAG for classified/unclassified stores with deterministic latency and provable data isolation.

Key constraints and asks (shorthand):
- Must run in air-gapped on-prem datacenters behind agency firewall; control plane can be private (customer-managed) or hosted in gov-cloud if required.
- Indexing scale: plan to onboard ~30M documents initially, expected growth to 100M+ within 18 months. Concerned about embedding generation cost and storage for dense vectors.
- Latency SLOs: 95th percentile under 300ms for typical query (embedding + rerank), target p50 < 120ms. Most queries are short chat-like queries and doc retrieval.
- Cost sensitivity: procurement expects unit economics analysis (cost/query) for hosted vs dedicated vs private. Requested cost model templates.
- Model preferences: open-model-first (LLaMA-family and RLHF variants), quantized weights acceptable; want option for certified model variants for classified workloads.
- Routing/fallback: need deterministic fallback to CPU-based sparse search if GPU pool unavailable; explicit policy controls for priority routing.
- Security/compliance: strict KMS/HSM integration, FIPS-validated crypto, audit logs with 7+ year retention, SSO via SAML + SCIM, per-route access controls.

Quotes: "We cannot tolerate silent data exfiltration risk — every cross-host call must be auditable," — CISO.

POC ask: deliver an on-prem pilot (air-gapped) for 5M-doc index, evaluate embed throughput and reranking latency, and provide a cost-per-query baseline. SE to propose batching and cache strategies to hit cost/latency targets.

Artifacts: pricing deck, security FAQ, and initial SOW drafts located in linked_drive_docs. Ongoing thread in gmail-thread-2025-01-21-BX9 contains procurement Qs.

Next tactical: finalize SOW and build on-prem pilot timeline. Procurement expects vendor questionnaire answers before demo to committee.

title: Astraeus Realtime Labs
preview:
Overview: enterprise quant trading shop building low-latency signal layer. Team: CTO (Victor Hale), Head of Infra (Rina Patel). We kicked off Dedicated evaluation 2024-09.

Key ask: deterministic tail latency <6ms p50/p95 for short completion paths; batchable work for embeddings & rerank off-path. Heavy throughput during market open windows (13:30-14:30 ET) - want reserved capacity to avoid noisy-neighbor impact.

SLA negotiation highlights: they insist on financial credits for downtime (tiered), 99.95% as baseline target proposed by them, pushing for 99.99% for core inference path. Must have <1 minute failover to fallback model variant. Require 72-hour maintenance notice, 2x per month maintenance window only outside 13:00-15:00 ET.

Security/compliance: strict KMS for keys, audit log retention 2 years, SSO via SAML + SCIM sync. Data residency: logs to remain in us-east region only. Legal wants right to audit infra configurations quarterly.

Procurement notes (shorthand): "legal: credit schedule, termination on 2 consecutive SLA breaches, cap on credits = monthly fee"; procurement wants sample contract redlines from Redwood.

Quote from CTO on 2024-11-21 call: "We need predictability — if it slips during opens we lose millions. Credits are good, but we need guaranteed reserve and fast remediation."

Attachments: pricing deck v3, rough perf-run CSV, SOC2 gap analysis spreadsheet.
